title: Dortmanna cliffortiana
intro: Dortmanna cliffortiana, also known as American or American waterdock, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America, characterized by its distinctive rounded leaves and stem. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna cliffortiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and as a diuretic to support urinary health.
taxonomy: Dortmanna cliffortiana is classified as a plant species within the family Haloragaceae. This plant is native to New Zealand and is also known as the 'New Zealand water-dilly-dally'. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is a monotypic genus. The plant is an aquatic species that thrives in freshwater environments. Dortmanna cliffortiana is a rare and endangered species.
